description: 2001 attacks in the United States
description_wiki: The September 11 attacks, colloquially known as 9/11, were a coordinated series of suicide attacks perpetrated by the Islamist terrorist organization al-Qaeda against the United States in 2001.

entity: Surgeon General of the United States Navy
description: most senior commissioned officer of the Medical Corps of the United States Navy
description_wiki: The surgeon general of the Navy (SGN) is the most senior commissioned officer of the Medical Corps of the United States Navy and is the principal advisor to the United States secretary of the navy, chief of naval operations and director of the defense health agency on all health and medical matters pertaining to the United States Navy and United States Marine Corps.

surface_forms: the World Trade Center in New York City
description: former skyscraper complex in Manhattan, New York (1968-2001)
description_wiki: The original World Trade Center (WTC) was a complex of seven buildings in the Financial District of Lower Manhattan in New York City.

aliases: Headquarters of the United States Department of Defense
Headquarters of the United States Department of War (2025–present)
Headquarters of the United States Department of Defense (1947–2025)
Headquarters of the United States Department of War (1789–1947)
description: headquarters of the United States Department of Defense; named after the shape of the building, a polygon with five (greek: penta) sides
description_wiki: The Pentagon is the headquarters of the United States Department of Defense, in Arlington County, Virginia, across the Potomac River from Washington, D.C. The building was constructed on an accelerated schedule during World War II. As a symbol of the U.S. military, the phrase The Pentagon is often used as a metonym for the Department of Defense and its leadership.

aliases: US Navy Bureau of Medicine and Surgery
U.S. Navy Bureau of Medicine and Surgery
Navy Bureau of Medicine and Surgery
description: agency of the United States Department of the Navy
description_wiki: The Bureau of Medicine and Surgery (BUMED) is an agency of the United States Department of the Navy that manages health care activities for the United States Navy and the United States Marine Corps.

surface_forms: National Naval Medical Center in Bethesda
description: tri-service U.S. military medical center in Bethesda, Maryland
description_wiki: Walter Reed National Military Medical Center (WRNMMC; formerly known as the National Naval Medical Center and colloquially referred to as Bethesda Naval Hospital, Walter Reed, or Navy Med) is a United States military medical center located in Bethesda, Maryland.

description: American publisher
description_wiki: Kathleen Martin is an American small press publisher, book editor and actor.

description: 1976 Mercy-class hospital ship
US government medical research agency
description_wiki: The National Institutes of Health (NIH) is the primary agency of the United States federal government responsible for biomedical and public health research.

entity: Chief of Chaplains of the United States Navy
surface_forms: Chief of Chaplains Office at the Navy Annex
description: position in the U.S. Navy
description_wiki: The chief of chaplains of the United States Navy (CHC) is the highest-ranking military chaplain in the United States Navy and head of the United States Navy Chaplain Corps.

description: American naval region
description_wiki: The Naval District Washington, one of eleven current naval regions, is responsible to Commander, Navy Installations Command for the operation and management of Naval shore installations in the Washington D.C. Metropolitan Area.

description: former shipyard and ordnance plant of the United States Navy
description_wiki: The Washington Navy Yard (WNY) is a ceremonial and administrative center for the United States Navy, located in the federal national capital city of Washington, D.C. (federal District of Columbia).

description: twinjet airliner family
description_wiki: The Boeing 767 is an American wide-body airliner developed and manufactured by Boeing Commercial Airplanes.

description: airliner family (1982–2005)
description_wiki: The Boeing 757 is an American narrow-body airliner designed and built by Boeing Commercial Airplanes.

surface_forms: honor guard that serves Arlington National Cemetery

description: national cemetery in Virginia, United States
description_wiki: Arlington National Cemetery is the largest cemetery in the United States National Cemetery System, one of two maintained by the United States Army.

aliases: United States Federal Emergency Management Agency
description: United States disaster response agency, part of Department of Homeland Security
description_wiki: The Federal Emergency Management Agency (FEMA) is an agency of the United States Department of Homeland Security (DHS), initially created under President Jimmy Carter by Presidential Reorganization Plan No.

description: maritime land force service branch of the United States Armed Forces
description_wiki: The United States Marine Corps (USMC), also referred to as the United States Marines or simply the Marines, is the naval infantry service branch of the United States Armed Forces.

aliases: Ground forces of the United States Armed Forces
description: land service branch of the United States Armed Forces
description_wiki: The United States Army (U.S. Army) is the land service branch of the United States Armed Forces.

description: air service branch of the United States Armed Forces
description_wiki: The United States Air Force (USAF) is the air service branch of the United States Armed Forces.

aliases: Red Cross of United States of America
United States of America Red Cross
description: National Red Cross Society in the United States
description_wiki: The American National Red Cross, sometimes referred to as ANRC, is an American nonprofit humanitarian organization that provides emergency assistance, disaster relief, and disaster preparedness education in the United States.

description: Evangelical Christian church and charitable organisation
American fast food restaurant chain
description_wiki: McDonald's Corporation, doing business as McDonald's, is an American multinational fast food restaurant chain.

description: global chain of hamburger fast food restaurants headquartered in Florida, United States
description_wiki: Burger King Corporation (BK, stylized in all caps) is an American fast food chain of hamburger fast food restaurants, headquartered in Miami-Dade County, Florida.

aliases: The City So Nice They Named It Twice
description: most populous city in the United States
description_wiki: New York, often called New York City (NYC), is the most populous city in the United States.

description: state of the United States of America
description_wiki: Tennessee ( , locally ), officially the State of Tennessee, is a landlocked state in the Southeastern region of the United States.

description: state of the United States of America
description_wiki: New Jersey is a state located in both the Mid-Atlantic and Northeastern regions of the United States.

aliases: The town of Our Lady the Queen of the Angels of the Little Portion
description: seat of Los Angeles County, and largest city in California, United States
description_wiki: Los Angeles (often referred to by its initials, LA) is the most populous city in the U.S. state of California, and the commercial, financial, and cultural center of Southern California.
